title: Introduction: Who is Golden Gate Manor?
description: An introduction to Gridlock & Gridlines and the mission behind Golden Gate Manor Inc.—serving Southern Colorado through transportation, assisted living, medical supplies, and community-based support.
fullDescription: Zach and Oscar introduce Gridlock & Gridlines and answer the big question: Who is Golden Gate Manor Inc.? Based in Pueblo and serving Southern Colorado, Golden Gate Manor Inc. is a family of services built around one mission—show up for the community. We talk about why this podcast exists, how our different lines of service connect through access and everyday support, and what listeners can expect as we explore local realities from different perspectives.

title: Protecting Rural Transportation in Colorado | The Fight for SB26-063
description: A discussion on rural transportation challenges in Colorado and the impact of broker-based Medicaid systems on local providers.
fullDescription: In this episode, we sit down with guests from the San Luis Valley, Red Willows (Mark and Kindra), to discuss the real challenges facing rural transportation providers in Colorado with a broker-based system. We break down the impact of broker-based systems in Medicaid transportation and why many rural operators struggle under centralized control. From dispatching and compliance to scheduling and patient care, local providers understand their communities in ways outside systems cannot replicate. Senate Bill 26-063 represents a critical step toward preserving independent operations, allowing providers to maintain accountability, reliability, and service quality for the people who depend on them. This conversation highlights the difference between systems designed from a distance and services built within the communities they serve.
